responses.text: The target text, published on the alternative health and conspiracy site Natural News by Mike Adams, accurately states that a Coca-Cola partner imports coca leaves but baselessly speculates that the refined cocaine byproduct is used for illicit bribes rather than medical purposes.

The core logistical premise of the article is factually correct: the New Jersey-based Stepan Company legally imports coca leaves from South America under special authorization from the Drug Enforcement Administration.

Stepan processes these leaves to produce a decocainized flavor extract that is sold exclusively to the Coca-Cola Company, a legacy arrangement designed to preserve the traditional taste of the beverage without retaining any narcotics.
During this extraction process, the active narcotic component is isolated and sold as pure cocaine to Mallinckrodt Pharmaceuticals, the only U.S. company licensed to receive it.

While the Natural News article strongly insinuates that the hundreds of kilos of this resulting cocaine cannot possibly be intended for legitimate medical use, refined cocaine actually has a long-standing, FDA-approved role in modern healthcare.

Because it acts simultaneously as a highly effective topical anesthetic and a potent vasoconstrictor, cocaine hydrochloride is routinely utilized in hospital settings to reduce bleeding and manage localized pain during ear, nose, and throat surgeries.

Therefore, while the target piece builds its narrative upon an accurate and historically unique corporate supply chain, it misleadingly characterizes standard pharmaceutical manufacturing as a nefarious, systemic conspiracy.
